series_metadata.series_description: The trial judge of any court of record shall suspend or revoke the driver's license of any person who is convicted, or pleads guilty to, any violation as listed in section 4507.16 of the Ohio Revised Code. The Conviction Cases are records of convictions or bond forveitures (Form UTT - Uniform Traffic Ticket). The tickets contain the violator's name and address, license number, date, location, type of violation, court's findings, sentence imposed and conviction date. The cases also contain insurance certificates or bonds evidencing financial responsibility and all correspondence relating thereto. These documents combine to establish cases which are scanned onto the optical imaging system in the Safety Responsibility Division of the Bureau. NOTE: Effective March 16, 1983, any person of a second offense D.U.I. (driving under the influence of alcohol and/or controlled substances) or any first time major conviction as specified under section 4507.16 of the Ohio Revised Code, is required to pay a reinstatement fee of $125.00 and maintain proof of financial responsibility insurance for 3 years from the date of suspension.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Paper: Effective September 1990 all cases generated will be recorded onto optical imaging system. All documents are scanned onto optical imaging system, reviewed ; Microform: Any cases generated prior January 1989 will remain on microfilm/microfiche if not updated onto optical imaging system. microfiche will not be duplicated storage at . cases are retained three (3) years from date of suspension ; Electronic: disks will be duplicated and stored at . case will be retained three (3) years from date of suspension
retention_rules.disposition_method: Paper: Wastepaper ; Microform: Burn ; Electronic: Erase
